title: gompholobium asperulum
intro: Gompholobium asperulum, also known as the rough pea-flower, is a shrubby perennial plant native to Australia, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive seed pods. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Gompholobium asperulum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, including skin irritations and respiratory infections.
